commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed because the PR combines broad operator-facing API work, missing contributor-owned real behavior proof, and maintainer decisions about diagnostic redaction, command shape, and upgrade/operator beha...; Needs attention: The patch introduces a diagnostic JSON surface that needs redaction before raw incident data is exposed to operators and support workflows.; Review finding: [P1] Redact incident... (sha=588a537ea42a450a760b105bf42187718aedb2c3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds control-plane hardening surfaces for incidents, baselines, probe caching, plugin contract validation, custom service wrapper reporting, and a new routed `openclaw diagnose --json` CLI command.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review findings by source inspection: current main exposes `status` plus `tasks.list/get/cancel`, while the PR calls `gateway.status` and `tasks.flows`, and the probe cache rejected-branch maps failures without preserving the item index. I did not run the PR branch because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the PR combines broad operator-facing API work, missing contributor-owned real behavior proof, and maintainer decisions about diagnostic redaction, command shape, and upgrade/operator behavior.

target.title: Control-plane hardening from Optimus ops findings
target.body:
## Summary

Completes the OpenClaw control-plane hardening project stack from Optimus ops findings:

- ADP-237: structured A2A handoff IDs and return-route tracing
- ADP-238: native incident ledger and repair audit trail
- ADP-239: regression baseline capture and postflight compare
- ADP-240: staggered channel startup with isolated probe cache
- ADP-241: plugin contract validator and startup diagnostics surface
- ADP-242: expected custom service wrapper config/reporting
- ADP-243: unified `openclaw diagnose --json` control-plane API

## Validation

- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/incidents/ledger.test.ts src/plugins/contract-validator.test.ts src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins contracts validate --strict --json` -> `{ok:true, pluginCount:125, findingCount:30}`
- `timeout 30s node scripts/run-node.mjs diagnose --json --timeout 1000` -> `{ok:true, openIncidents:0, taskErrors:0, configuredChannels:2}`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-channel-surface.config.ts src/channels/plugins/contracts/plugin.registry-backed-shard-e.contract.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.bundled.config.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts -t "supports legacy plugins subscribing to diagnostic events from the root sdk"`
- `pnpm test:changed:max` progressed through unit-fast, unit, boundary, tooling, channel contracts, plugin contracts, and bundled shards; stale upstream test expectations were fixed in this branch.
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

The upstream repository rejected direct push for the active account, so this PR is opened from the accessible `fchaudhryspear/openclaw` fork. The branch has been rebased onto current upstream `main` and GitHub reports it as mergeable.
target.author: fchaudhryspear
